description_long: This is a weathered, metallic cup with a cylindrical shape and a ribbed midsection, giving it a rugged, industrial appearance. The surface is predominantly gray with visible dents, scratches, and signs of rust, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. One side of the cup features an unusual attachment: a rough, brownish panel secured with metal rods and bolts, possibly a makeshift repair or reinforcement. The interior appears metallic and uncoated, matching the exterior's utilitarian look. The cup is open at the top, with a slightly flared rim and a flat, stable base. Its overall design and condition suggest it may have been repurposed from a tin can or similar container, commonly found in urban or street environments.
description: A battered, ribbed metal cup with a rough brown panel bolted to one side, showing signs of rust and heavy use.
description_view_0: The cup's ribbed metal body and open top are clearly visible, with no attachments seen from this angle.
description_view_1: The side view reveals a brown, irregular panel attached with metal rods and bolts, protruding from the cup's surface.
description_view_2: The brown panel is fully visible, showing multiple dark holes and a rough texture, dominating this side of the cup.
description_view_3: The attachment is seen from the opposite side, with metal rods and bolts securing it to the cup, while the rest of the cup remains ribbed and metallic.
